title: acer monspessulanum var. ibericum
intro: Acer monspessulanum var. ibericum, also known as the Iberian maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the family Sapindaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Acer monspessulanum var. ibericum are said to include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
